did.unittitle: WHCU Department of Black Affairs records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: WHCU (Radio station : Ithaca, N.Y.). Department of Black Affairs

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: WHCU (Radio station : Ithaca, N.Y.). Department of Black Affairs

did.abstract: Tape recordings and scripts of the Night Sounds program broadcast on radio station WHCU in Ithaca, New York; interviews, promotional advertisements for local businesses, local news broadcasts, and other programs for the African American listening audience in Central New York State.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Tape recordings and scripts of the Night Sounds program broadcast on radio station WHCU in Ithaca, New York from 1974-1985. Includes promotional advertisements for local businesses; news broadcasts; programs for the African American listening audience in New York State, including African folktales for children and political issues; correspondence and fan mail; business files.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: WHCU Department of Black Affairs records, #4904. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collection 6580 was merged into this collection (March 2021).

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also WHCU radio station records, collection #35-9-1951.

Radio stations -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Afro-American radio stations -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Afro-American mass media -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Recordings were reformatted onto Audio Cassettes in ~1991. These audio cassetes are now held by Cornell's Africana Library as their Night Sounds collection. This series was re-described as part of digitization of material in 2021. Some reels have had multiple identifiers over time and holding location. These identifiers are added as processing notes to the item record to cross-reference old numbers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with Brother Felipe- History of Afro-Latino Music. He gives backgrounds on some of the most political Latino musicians. He talks about the relationship between African and Latin music. Includes some music sample of Cuban, Puerto Rican, Dominican, and other Hispanic music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with Noel Mukono, Secretary of Foreign Affairs of Zimbabwe African National Union (Zimbabwe, Rhodesia), who talks about the politics and government, social and health conditions of Zimbabwe. Rest of the News segment covers Ku Klux Klan killings; South Africa news brief; Local news

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farewell Reception for Lynn M. Harvey; Reception held at the Elks Nest-536 West Green Street in Ithaca. Lynn was Asst. Director at WHCU-Nightsound (Cornell U.)s from 1981-1984. did.unittitle: Reel 3: White Roots|David Sebiko, Pan-African Congress of Azainia (South Africa)

dao.xlink:title: Reel 3: White Roots|David Sebiko, Pan-African Congress of Azainia (South Africa) [Preservation]

daodesc.p: Reel 3: White Roots|David Sebiko, Pan-African Congress of Azainia (South Africa) [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detailed Interview with Sebiko- Just returning from appearing before the security council of the United Nations. Outlines of the Azania liberation movements agenda. Speaks on how the Western Media have undermined the anti-apartheid movement and have supported the minority government. He also points out the role that the United States government played in helping the minority regime stay in power.

did.unittitle: Black History Month 1984, week 1: Ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu, plus "Street Beat" with Ken Glover (9 individual interviews)

dao.xlink:title: Black History Month 1984, week 1: Ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu, plus "Street Beat" with Ken Glover (9 individual interviews) [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: Black History Month 1984, week 1: Ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu, plus "Street Beat" with Ken Glover (9 individual interviews) [Staff access]

daodesc.p: Black History Month 1984, week 1: Ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu, plus "Street Beat" with Ken Glover (9 individual interviews) [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History of ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu; Black Womanhood in celebration of Black History Month-Understanding the importance of women in the Black community and the family. Focus on her strength and spirit. Overview of some former African Queens; 1. Hatshepsut (5:27 min.) Ruled Ancient Egypt during the 17th dynasty. 2. Queen of Sheba-Makeda, ruler of Ethiopia. 3. Cleopatra-Queen of Egypt, became a goddess. 4. Queen of Zehobia, biographical stetch. 5. Queen Nzinga of Southern Africa.–Street Beat with Ken Glover, individual interviews.

daodesc.p: Interview with Dr. Ibrahim Abdul-Malik [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explanation of the Summer Academy program in NYC. This program was designed to expose inner city younsters who might not have had the opportunity to be exposed to college. Dr. Abdulmalik gave some history on the program and explained the importance of keeping this program alive. The main focus is about ways to encourage and to give high school students the assistance they need to get a higher education.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion on the problems in Dominica. Sandy Black host; George Nelson-guest. Nelson was a graduate student at Cornell who was a native Dominican. He spoke of the struggles that were going on in Dominica and how the people were organizing to deal with overcoming those problems.

dao.xlink:title: Focus: Malcolm X [Preservation]

daodesc.p: Focus: Malcolm X [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: Focus: Malcolm X [Staff access]

daodesc.p: Focus: Malcolm X [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Biography sketch on Malcolm X. Looks at Malcolm X's early childhood. Deals with the early influences in his life, which included his father and the UNIA (Universal Negro Improvement Association). After Malcolm's father was killed, his mother was placed in a mental hospital. Once Malcolm reached early adulthood he lived with his sister at Boston and turned into a life of crime. He eventually landed in prison where the teachings of Elijah Muhammad and the Nation of Islam were introduced to him. upon leaving prison Malcolm joined the Nation and became its spokes person.

did.unittitle: The State of Black America segment: Blacks in the military, part II with Fred Ennis

dao.xlink:title: The State of Black America segment: Blacks in the military, part II with Fred Ennis [Preservation]

daodesc.p: The State of Black America segment: Blacks in the military, part II with Fred Ennis [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: The State of Black America segment: Blacks in the military, part II with Fred Ennis [Staff access]

daodesc.p: The State of Black America segment: Blacks in the military, part II with Fred Ennis [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Overview of Blacks role in American's battles. Focus on the wars against the Native Americans, Civil War, Spanish American War, World War One, World War Two, and the Vietnam War. There is also a look at the hypocrisy of American policy in which Jim Crow was enforced; Blacks fought and died in the name of America, but were still lynched once they returned to the United States. There is also discussion about the economic reasons why Blacks join the military in disproportionate numbers.

dao.xlink:title: Black World News with Arnold Stovell [Preservation]

daodesc.p: Black World News with Arnold Stovell [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: Black World News with Arnold Stovell [Staff access]

daodesc.p: Black World News with Arnold Stovell [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1)Filipino General Marcos takes a leave–Oct. 15, 1985 2)Ebony insights–The Greying of Black music–Detailing the importance of music within the Black community. Blacks should be less concerned with trying to crossover in order to appeal to white audience. 3)Ebony insights–Blues for Black actors–Blacks are under represented in cinema. Most successful are comics e.g. Richard Pryor, Eddie Murphy. 4)Mondale retires, will return to D.C. and practice law. 5)State of Black America Report–1985–Blacks made progress in the 1960's; peaked in the 1970's; and have fallen off ever since. Wide gap between Black and Whites in earnings.

Scott King praises Gov. scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1) Latin peace talks cancelled–Feb. 14, 1985 2) Shultz accuses Nicaragua of falling behind Iran curtain– Feb. 20, 1985 3) One hundred and forty eight are killed as Spanish Jet hits mountain (double-dub) Feb. 20, 1985 4) Ethiopian Jews forcefully removed to Israel–Feb. 27, 1985 5) Delegation of five bishops visit Nicaragua Feb.27, 1985 6) Summary of Goetz case–March 2, 1985 7) Israeli jets attack guerilla bases in Lebanon–11/25/84 8) Kenya President wants government to play his tune–11/3/84 9) Ethiopia gets new aid from U.S.–Oct. 26, 1984 10) Violation ruled out when restaurant owner refuses to serve Blacks–Dec. 1, 1984 11) Ethiopia guerilla release 3 hostages taken 6 weeks ago 12) Honduras to open a new U.S. base–Dec. 1, 1984 13) Human rights in Haiti–March 13,1984 14) Two congressmen held after South African embassy protest- -Dec. 1, 1984 15) Goetz case reopened–March 13, 1985 16) Brazil tries to stop cocaine traffic–March 14, 1985 17) New witness for Goetz case–March 16, 1985 18) Grenada reports Economic revival–March 27, 1985 19) Trinidad adjusts it's view on the outside world–4/3/85 20) South Africans testify on the use of heavy ammunition– April 10, 1985 21)Haitians removed from high risk category for AIDS–4/10/85 22)Judge dismissed indictment of Police Officer Steven Sullivan in Eleanor Bumpurs killing–April 17, 1985

did.unittitle: African Folk Tales: The Enchanted Buck

dao.xlink:title: African Folk Tales: The Enchanted Buck [Preservation]

daodesc.p: African Folk Tales: The Enchanted Buck [Preservation]

dao.xlink:title: African Folk Tales: The Enchanted Buck [Staff access]

daodesc.p: African Folk Tales: The Enchanted Buck [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: African Folk Tales-The Enchanted Buck. A young bride is accused of being a witch by her groom's father when the kill for her wedding feast turns into a white buck. The groom's father calls the wedding off and the bride goes back to the village where she sees the buck again. She follows the buck into a forest in which a hungry ogre grabs for the buck and when he does so, the buck turns into a handsome chief. The chief asks the young woman to be his bride. The moral of the story is that people should not always kill animals to eat for pleasure.
